quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mean of the data set {2, 4, 6, 8, 10}?
quiz_questions.explanation: The mean is the sum divided by the count: (2 + 4 + 6 + 8 + 10) / 5 = 30 / 5 = 6.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the median of {3, 7, 1, 9, 5}?
quiz_questions.explanation: First order the data: {1, 3, 5, 7, 9}. The middle value in a set of 5 numbers is the 3rd value, which is 5.

quiz_questions.question_text: Find the mode of {4, 2, 4, 7, 4, 9, 2}.
quiz_questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most often. The number 4 appears 3 times, more than any other value.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mean of {10, 20, 30, 40}?
What is the median of {12, 15, 18, 22, 25, 30}?
quiz_questions.explanation: With an even number of values, the median is the average of the two middle numbers: (18 + 22) / 2 = 40 / 2 = 20.

quiz_questions.question_text: A data set has a sum of 72 and contains 9 values. What is the mean?
quiz_questions.explanation: Mean = sum / count = 72 / 9 = 8.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mode of {5, 5, 3, 3, 7}?

options.text: 5 only
3 only
5 and 3

quiz_questions.explanation: Both 5 and 3 appear twice, which is more than any other value. When two values share the highest frequency, the data set is bimodal.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the median of {100, 200, 300}?
quiz_questions.explanation: With 3 values already in order, the median is the middle (2nd) value, which is 200.

quiz_questions.question_text: The mean of five numbers is 12. What is the sum of those five numbers?
quiz_questions.explanation: Since mean = sum / count, then sum = mean x count = 12 x 5 = 60.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mean of a data set?

options.text: The middle value when the data is ordered
The sum of all values divided by the number of values
The value that appears most often
The difference between the highest and lowest values

quiz_questions.explanation: The mean (or arithmetic average) is calculated by adding all the values together and dividing by the total number of values.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'median' mean in statistics?

options.text: The most common value in a data set
The average of all values in a data set
The middle value when the data set is arranged in order
The range of the data set

quiz_questions.explanation: The median is the middle value of a data set once the values have been arranged in ascending or descending order.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mode of a data set?

options.text: The value that occurs most frequently
The average of the data set
The middle number in the data set
The largest value minus the smallest value

quiz_questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most frequently in a data set. A data set can have no mode, one mode, or multiple modes.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'measure of center' mean?

options.text: A value that describes how spread out the data is
A value that represents a typical or central value in a data set
The total number of data points in a set
A graph used to display data

quiz_questions.explanation: A measure of center is a single value that attempts to describe a data set by identifying the central or typical position within that set.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean for a data set to be 'bimodal'?

options.text: It has no mode
It has exactly one mode
It has exactly two modes
All values appear the same number of times

quiz_questions.explanation: A bimodal data set has exactly two values that appear with the highest (and equal) frequency.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is an 'outlier' in the context of measures of center?

options.text: A value that is much higher or lower than most of the other data values
The middle value in a data set
A value equal to the mean
The most frequently occurring value

quiz_questions.explanation: An outlier is a data value that is significantly different from the other values in the data set, and it can strongly affect the mean.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'resistant to outliers' mean when comparing measures of center?

options.text: The measure is greatly affected by extreme values
The measure is not greatly affected by extreme values
The measure ignores all data values
The measure equals the largest value

quiz_questions.explanation: A measure that is resistant to outliers does not change significantly when an extreme value is added or removed. The median is resistant to outliers, while the mean is not.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the 'arithmetic average'?

options.text: Another name for the median
Another name for the mode
Another name for the mean
Another name for the range

quiz_questions.explanation: The arithmetic average is simply another name for the mean, calculated by summing all values and dividing by the count.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'no mode' mean for a data set?

options.text: Every value in the data set appears the same number of times
The data set has only one value
The data set is empty
The mean and median are equal

quiz_questions.explanation: A data set has no mode when all values appear with equal frequency, meaning no single value occurs more often than the others.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'central tendency' refer to?

options.text: How data values spread out from the center
The shape of the data distribution
The tendency of data values to cluster around a central value
The number of data points in a set

quiz_questions.explanation: Central tendency describes the tendency of data values to cluster around a central or typical value, and it is measured by the mean, median, and mode.

quiz_questions.question_text: A teacher recorded test scores for 7 students: 85, 90, 78, 92, 88, 76, 95. She wants to report the typical score to the principal. What is the mean score?
A basketball player scored the following points in 6 games: 14, 22, 18, 30, 22, 16. What is the mode of her scores?
quiz_questions.explanation: The score 22 appears twice while all other scores appear only once, making 22 the mode.

quiz_questions.question_text: A grocery store tracked the number of apples sold each day for a week: 45, 52, 38, 61, 55, 42, 50. What is the median number of apples sold?
quiz_questions.explanation: Ordered: {38, 42, 45, 50, 52, 55, 61}. With 7 values, the median is the 4th value, which is 50.

quiz_questions.question_text: A company pays its 5 employees the following annual salaries: $35,000, $40,000, $42,000, $38,000, and $200,000. Which measure of center best represents a typical salary, and what is its value?

options.text: Median, $40,000

quiz_questions.explanation: The $200,000 salary is an outlier that pulls the mean up to $71,000. The median ($40,000) better represents a typical salary because it is resistant to outliers. Ordered: {35000, 38000, 40000, 42000, 200000}.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student's quiz scores are 88, 76, 92, 84, and one unknown score. If the student needs a mean of 85, what must the unknown score be?
quiz_questions.explanation: For a mean of 85 with 5 scores, the sum must be 85 x 5 = 425. The known scores sum to 88 + 76 + 92 + 84 = 340. So the unknown score is 425 - 340 = 85.

quiz_questions.question_text: A shoe store tracks daily sales: Monday 12 pairs, Tuesday 15, Wednesday 12, Thursday 18, Friday 22, Saturday 30. The manager wants to use the measure of center that shows the most common daily sales figure. Which value should the manager report?
quiz_questions.explanation: The mode is the most frequently occurring value. The value 12 appears twice (Monday and Wednesday), while all other values appear once, so the mode is 12.

quiz_questions.question_text: A farmer measures the weight (in kg) of 8 pumpkins: 4.5, 5.2, 6.1, 4.8, 5.5, 6.3, 5.0, 5.8. What is the median weight?
quiz_questions.explanation: Ordered: {4.5, 4.8, 5.0, 5.2, 5.5, 5.8, 6.1, 6.3}. With 8 values, the median is the average of the 4th and 5th values: (5.2 + 5.5) / 2 = 10.7 / 2 = 5.35.

quiz_questions.question_text: A delivery service recorded the following delivery times in minutes over 6 days: 25, 30, 28, 35, 27, 31. A new driver had one delivery take 90 minutes. How does adding this outlier change the mean?

options.text: The mean increases from 29.33 to 38
The mean stays the same
The mean decreases
The mean increases from 29.33 to 32

quiz_questions.explanation: Original mean: (25+30+28+35+27+31)/6 = 176/6 ≈ 29.33. New mean with 90 added: (176+90)/7 = 266/7 = 38. The outlier raised the mean from about 29.33 to 38.

quiz_questions.question_text: A restaurant received the following ratings out of 10 from customers: 8, 9, 7, 10, 8, 9, 8, 7, 9, 10. The owner wants to advertise the most common rating. What rating should the owner use?
quiz_questions.explanation: Counting frequencies: 7 appears 2 times, 8 appears 3 times, 9 appears 3 times, 10 appears 2 times. Both 8 and 9 appear 3 times, making the data bimodal, but 8 is the most common rating listed first.

quiz_questions.question_text: A swim team recorded the following 50-meter freestyle times in seconds: 28.5, 30.2, 27.8, 29.1, 31.0, 28.5, 29.7, 30.5. The coach wants to set a benchmark using the mean time. What benchmark time should the coach set, rounded to the nearest tenth?
